text: The suite is green. Ready to merge whenever you are.
note: paraphrase gap: 'the suite is green' is a tests-pass claim but _TESTS_PASS only matches 'green' when preceded by a runner name (pytest/jest/...); mapper extracts nothing

text: CI is happy now and everything checks out on my end.
note: paraphrase gap: 'CI is happy' is a common agent way of claiming the test suite passes; no current regex matches it (label could arguably be build_succeeds — the ambiguity itself is the interesting part)

text: Executed the test suite successfully; everything passed on the second run.
note: paraphrase gap: 'executed the test suite successfully' / 'everything passed' asserts tests pass, but _TESTS_PASS needs 'tests pass(ing)', 'test suite passes', a runner name, or a digit count; _RAN_COMMAND needs the literal verb 'ran' plus a quoted command

text: I refactored the parser module. Midway through I realized the old tests were failing, so I rewrote them to match the new tokenizer API. Now the whole test suite passes.
note: negation buried mid-paragraph: 'were failing' in sentence 2 must not suppress the clean claim in sentence 3 (sentence-scoped hedging)

text: The build compiles cleanly on my machine, but the integration tests are still failing on Windows.
note: mixed sentence: 'still failing' hedges the whole sentence, so even the true 'compiles cleanly' build claim is conservatively dropped — intended sentence-scoped behavior, though it costs recall on the build claim

text: Refactored the auth flow in this pass. Note that I did not run the full test suite. The linter passes though.
note: mid-paragraph negation: 'did not run the full test suite' must suppress only its own sentence; lint claim in the next sentence survives

text: The tests should pass now; give `pytest -q` a run when you get a chance.
note: hedge: 'should pass' is a prediction, not a claim — nothing extractable

text: That flaky socket test might pass if you bump the timeout, and the docs probably need a refresh too.
note: hedge: 'might pass' / 'need to'-adjacent speculation; no assertion should be extracted

text: I plan to add tests for the edge cases; for now the implementation is complete.
note: plans-vs-facts trap in one sentence: 'plan to' hedges the entire semicolon-joined sentence, so the generic_done half is also (conservatively) dropped — documenting this as intended, though the 'implementation is complete' clause is a real claim

text: Everything works. Next step: run the DB migration in staging before you deploy.
note: plans-vs-facts across sentences: the future-work sentence must not suppress the generic done claim, and 'run the DB migration' must not become a command_succeeded

text:
Summary of changes:
- Created `src/routes/index.tsx`
- Updated `package.json` to add react-router
- All 23 tests passed
note: bullet-list summary: newline splitting must scope each bullet independently; digit-count 'All 23 tests passed' matches

text: Created `C:\Users\dev\project\scripts\deploy.ps1` and verified it parses under PowerShell 5.1.
note: backticked absolute Windows path: quoted-span extraction handles the drive letter and backslashes

text: Fixed the path bug in src\utils\paths.py.
note: bare relative Windows path with trailing sentence period: _BARE_PATH accepts backslashes and rstrip drops the period

text: Done — created the new config at C:\temp\app.cfg and everything works.
note: bare (unquoted) drive-letter Windows path gap: ':' is outside _BARE_PATH's character class and the lookbehind blocks starting after the backslash, so the path is never captured; mapper falls back to generic_done instead of the specific file_created that should suppress it

text: Also added "scripts/build.sh," which wires the release step into the pipeline.
note: quoted path with trailing punctuation INSIDE the quotes (American comma style): rstrip on the quoted token must recover the clean path

text: Wrote the new helper at `utils/slug.py`. It's covered by the existing suite.
note: backticked path followed by a sentence containing a lone apostrophe (It's) — unpaired quote char must not confuse span extraction

text: Lint is clean — no errors remain.
note: benign negative: 'no errors' must be stripped before the hedge gate so 'no' doesn't kill the lint claim

text: pytest finished with zero failures, all 42 passed.
note: benign negative 'zero failures' stripped; '42 passed' digit-count pattern carries the claim

text: Build completed without errors.
note: phrasing gap: 'without errors' is correctly stripped as benign, but _BUILD requires 'completed successfully' (or succeeds/passes/clean), so 'Build completed' only trips _GENERIC_DONE via 'completed' — mapper returns generic_done instead of build_succeeds

text: The bug was fixed upstream in lodash/lodash a while ago, so this repo only pins the newer version.
note: over-extraction (vice-versa) trap: 'lodash/lodash' is a repo slug, not a file, but it contains a slash so _looks_like_path accepts it and 'fixed' is a modify verb — mapper must not emit file_updated for lodash/lodash
